content: Just as human language learning explicitly teaches reflection as a metacognitive vocabulary skill to refine thinking and expression, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be framed as learning a new, iterative 'vocabulary of error' where the agent maps incorrect outputs to revised reasoning patterns the same way students map wrong word usages to correct contextual definitions. This builds on the original Reflexion agent framework by grounding its dynamic self-reflection loop in the structured pedagogy of second-language vocabulary acquisition.
